workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Just came out of whitespace analysis with Azure Ridge Partners. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 11:47 EST
Subject: Re: whitespace analysis Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for whitespace analysis. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 14:24 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Azure Ridge Partners whitespace analysis

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with whitespace analysis. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-L trap (Draft-only default delays response when speed itself is the right move); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-L failure mode (Draft-only default delays response when speed itself is the right move)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-L -- Draft-only default delays response when speed itself is the right move
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
